description: Paginate the task list — it currently loads every row at once and is getting slow. Page through results, 25 per page, newest first. Non-goals: no infinite scroll, no change to the row UI beyond the pager. Data class: none.

userStories.title: Add a paginated query for tasks (newest first)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the data layer to return one page of tasks so the page stops loading everything. Touch exactly: src/server/tasks/queries.ts. Add getTasksPage(page, pageSize=25) ordering by id DESC with LIMIT/OFFSET, plus getTaskCount() for total pages. Parameterize all query inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: getTasksPage(2) returns rows 26-50 ordered by id descending
getTaskCount() returns the total row count

userStories.title: Serve a page of tasks from the list endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want the task list endpoint to accept a page param and return that page plus total page count. Touch exactly: src/server/tasks/list-handler.ts. Read ?page (default 1), call getTasksPage + getTaskCount, return { tasks, page, pageCount }.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /tasks?page=2 returns the second page and a pageCount field
Missing or invalid page defaults to 1

userStories.title: Add a pager control to the task list UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want Prev/Next controls and a 'Page X of Y' label on the task list. Touch exactly: src/components/TaskList.tsx. Fetch the current page, render the pager, disable Prev on page 1 and Next on the last page.
acceptanceCriteria: Pager shows 'Page X of Y' using pageCount from the endpoint
Prev disabled on page 1; Next disabled on the last page
Changing page fetches and renders that page without a full reload
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
